content.boundary: Applies to the statutory age of first academic tracking and the legal status of 6-/8-year gimnázium entry points nationwide; excludes vocational-track content, higher-education admission, and general teacher-pay policy except where transition capacity requires it.
content.change_lever: Parliamentary amendment of Act CXC of 2011 to raise the statutory tracking age, phase out 6/8-year entry gates, and bundle the change with unified curriculum and anti-sorting school-choice/catchment rules, following the comparative pattern that delayed tracking succeeds only when bundled with structural reforms.
content.name: Statutory delay of first academic tracking to age 14+ with unified comprehensive lower-secondary phase
content.system_problem: Hungary selects at age 10-12, far earlier than the OECD average of 15, and this early tracking is empirically associated with the highest between-school segregation in the EU, one of the largest OECD socioeconomic achievement gaps, and structural entanglement with Roma ethnic segregation absent from comparable systems.
